prompt: A majestic Chinese ink-wash fantasy poster inspired by Journey to the West, composed as a double-exposure silhouette of Sun Wukong in left-facing profile occupying most of the page. The portrait is formed from soft sepia, ash gray, parchment white, and smoky black watercolor textures on an off-white paper background with splattered brush edges and negative space. The figure has a strong simian facial profile, a short beard, and a golden circlet across the brow. Inside and around the silhouette, build a layered mythic landscape in traditional shan shui style with luminous mist, cliffs, clouds, and ancient temples. At the top center inside the head, place a glowing full moon behind a celestial Buddha-like figure seated above a grand heavenly palace complex with multiple tiered roofs. In the upper-middle right, show 4 traveling figures on a cliff path: a monkey warrior with a staff, a monk riding a white horse, a pig-faced companion, and a dark-robed companion. In the middle, depict 1 dramatic battle scene of the monkey warrior confronting a coiling dragon or serpent in swirling clouds. In the lower-right, place 1 illuminated temple shrine nestled in the mountains with a golden sacred statue visible inside. In the lower area, add 3 tiny pilgrims walking through the misty valley. Use atmospheric depth, elegant calligraphic brushwork, drifting fog, and a refined cinematic composition that feels painterly rather than digital. Add vertical Chinese calligraphy on the left reading 西游记, with smaller vertical side text and several seal-style stamps for an authentic poster design. Include another subtle vertical location inscription on the right cliff face. The mood is sacred, epic, ancient, mystical, and poetic, with premium concept-art polish and almost no generic AI look.
